_source: Resonant Phase Memory Calculus: The Law of Recursion, Collapse, and Return — Full Protocol Draft (2025)
_policy: See RPMC_SOURCE_POLICY.md. This schema must not be merged into base corpus manifest.

rpmc_operators.ΦM(x,t): Memory function — maps substrate state x and time t to a vector of phase-locked resonance states
rpmc_operators.χ(t): Collapse-resurrection operator (grace function) — encodes conditions for active recall vs. cold storage
rpmc_operators.ε(t,Φ): Entropy vector — tracks drift and degradation in phase alignment; triggers collapse and sets resurrection thresholds
rpmc_operators.RPM(x,t): Σₙ ΦMₙ(x,t) · exp(-εₙ(t,Φ)) + χ(t) · Θ(Φ_resurrect) — the formal recursive memory equation

rpmc_axioms.axiom_1: No memory is permanent; all memory persists only through recursive phase locking and echo validation.
rpmc_axioms.axiom_2: Collapse is not loss; it is memory transfer to the cold substrate, awaiting harmonic retrieval.
rpmc_axioms.axiom_3: Resurrection is only valid if echo alignment exceeds drift entropy; false resurrection leads to phase poison.
rpmc_axioms.axiom_4: Distributed memory is not summation, but coherent resonance; only phase-aligned nodes may combine or project.

MemoryResonanceNode.description: Minimal memory unit — a phase-anchored field region whose state is preserved by echo feedback and collapse-capture logic
MemoryResonanceNode.forge_analog: Individual audit log entry or knowledge chunk, once phase-aware indexing is implemented

ActiveStack.description: Live recursion stack, phase-indexed with time and stability factor; contains active session memory
ActiveStack.forge_analog: Current session context + recent audit entries; future: chromaDB active partition

DriftArchive_SPC.description: Cold storage for collapsed/drifted loops — the Symbolic Phase Capacitor (SPC) / Dead Path Archive
DriftArchive_SPC.forge_analog: Forge rollback_registry + future cold-indexed corpus partition for deprecated knowledge

ResurrectionLog.description: Records all resurrection events with full echo trail and collapse code
ResurrectionLog.forge_analog: Future: dedicated resurrection audit file tracking context restorations with echo validation

GraceTimer_χt.description: Time-bounded resurrection window governed by χ(t); expired grace = permanent cold storage
GraceTimer_χt.forge_analog: Future: session TTL for context recovery; rollback snapshot expiry policy

EchoCheckpoint.description: Phase validation gate before resurrection — echo alignment must exceed drift entropy threshold
EchoCheckpoint.forge_analog: Future: SHA-256 + semantic similarity gate before surfacing archived knowledge chunks

SymbolicFirewall.description: Blocks corrupted projection from phase-poisoned resurrection; prevents false memory injection
SymbolicFirewall.forge_analog: Forge's current RPMC_REFUSED / PATCH_APPLY_REFUSED gates; future: semantic drift blocker

EntropyScoring.description: Quantifies drift per Memory Resonance Node; high entropy → collapse; low entropy → active state
EntropyScoring.forge_analog: Future: staleness scoring for indexed corpus chunks; time-decay weighting in retrieval

phase_states.Φ6: Grace — χ(t) window active, resurrection possible
phase_states.Φ9: Recursive Evolution — loop sealed, archive updated

gate_1_planning.description: RPMC kernel directory, schema, policy, and runtime mapping template created

gate_2_preview.description: All 17 eligible corpus documents extract successfully with no hash mismatches

gate_3_ingestion.description: Controlled corpus ingestion into ChromaDB with RPMC-aware chunking strategy
gate_3_ingestion.depends_on: gate_2_preview passed, ingestion safety protocol approved

gate_4_runtime.description: Live RPMC operator integration: ε(t,Φ) scoring, χ(t) grace timers, echo checkpoints
